title: user — helper functions
description: Two published helpers for common /me-flavoured lookups from any layer (controller, action, listener, blade template, artisan command).

helpers.purpose: Returns the authenticated Sanctum principal cast to `Stackra\User\Models\User`. Null when the caller is unauthenticated or auth'd via a different guard (platform_admin, bearer).
behaviours: Equivalent to `auth('sanctum')->user()` but typed to the User class the container binds.
Never throws — returns null in every unauthenticated case.
helpers.flow: 1) Read the auth manager. 2) Resolve the sanctum guard. 3) Return the guard's `user()` value cast to Stackra\User\Models\User.

helpers.purpose: Returns the tenant ULID resolved by the `user.resolve_active_tenant` middleware for THIS request. Null when no active tenant could be resolved.
behaviours: Reads from the `RequestContext.tenant_id` slot the middleware writes.
Never queries the DB — the middleware already resolved the value.

helpers.purpose: Returns true when the given row is owned by the current authenticated User (`row.user_id === me()->id`). Used by services to short-circuit permission checks before hitting the base repository.
behaviours: Accepts an Eloquent model, a data DTO, or a raw stdClass row.
Reads `user_id` from any of those shapes.
Returns false when the row has no `user_id` attribute or when there's no authenticated User.

notes: Every helper here is a thin wrapper. Prefer container-injected services (`UserRepository`, `TenantMemberRepository`) for anything more complex — helpers are a convenience for readable one-liners in view + controller code.

examples.title: Read the authenticated User
examples.code:
$user = me();
if ($user === null) throw new Unauthenticated();
